description:
GOAL: When the typer proves both operands of an arithmetic / ordered-comparison BinOp are Int, ir_codegen emits the inline fast path WITHOUT the both_int test. Recovers the runtime overhead introduced by .11.20's tag-dispatched arithmetic for typed Int code.

## Why this is atomic

Before: every BinOp::Add etc. emits 5 ops to test 'both Int' + a brif + a runtime helper call site that's branch-predicted but still occupies icache. After: typed-Int arithmetic is exactly the original .11.7 fast path (ai = sshr_imm 3; bi = sshr_imm 3; iadd; box_int). Mixed/Float code still pays the dispatch (correct). 337 existing tests stay green; new tests verify elision happens via inspecting compiled IR.

## Deliverables

src/ir_codegen.rs:
- compile() retrieves `var_types` from .11.24.3's typer pass (already attached to CompiledModule / Module per .11.24.2's design).
- compile_fn() threads var_types into lower_prim alongside env.
- BinOp::Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod and Lt/Le/Gt/Ge: peek at operand vars' Descrs. If both Descr <= int_top, skip emit_dispatch_binop; emit only the fast inline body. Else emit dispatch as today.
- BinOp::Eq/Neq: similar — if both vars Descr <= scalar_top (Int / Atom / Special; nothing heap-typed), skip the both_ptr test and emit only the icmp. (Optional polish; main gain is on arithmetic.)
- Helper: `fn descr_is_int(d: &Descr) -> bool` central truth for 'this Var is provably Int.'

## Tests

- Compile `fn add(a, b), do: a + b` and verify the emitted Cranelift IR (via Function::display) does NOT contain a call to fz_arith_add (params widen to int_top through fn-shape; arithmetic is provably Int+Int). Use a Cranelift IR string-contains assertion.
- Compile `fn mix(x), do: x + 2.0` and verify the slow path IS present (since x is Top).
- Existing arithmetic tests in ir_codegen pass byte-for-byte (halt values unchanged).
- Microbench (optional, separate test attribute): typed Int loop of 1M iterations vs same loop forced to take dispatch is at least 1.5x faster.

## Out of scope

- f64-fast-path (unboxed float arithmetic) when both operands are Float — needs separate ticket; this ticket only elides the dispatch on the int side.
- Const-pool for boxed floats.

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.24.3.
- BLOCKS: none directly. (Quality-of-implementation; functional correctness already in .11.20.)
title: ir_codegen consumer: arithmetic dispatch elision
